title: Abortion laws and women's health

original_result.source: Table 2, Panel A, Column 1 (no weights, no controls). SE is wild bootstrap.

notes: Single treated unit: estado=9 (Mexico DF) from time=207 (Mar 2007, matching Stata: timetoILE=t-207). ILE dummy in data turns on at time=205 (Jan 2007), but paper centers event study at t=207; gvar_CS=207 aligns with paper's definition. Previously gvar_CS=205 (Jan 2007) was 2 months too early. Pattern 43 fix applied: gvar_CS was integer (207L/0L) causing Inf→NA truncation inside did package — changed to numeric (207/0). 12 never-treated control states. SA disabled (single cohort). SEs in paper use wild bootstrap; template uses clustered SEs — expect SE differences. Time variable is running month index (1=Jan 1990). Stata Table 2 uses ALL years (1990-2016, N=2496) with regressiveState==0 filter (regressive states excluded). Previously year>=2001 filter caused 11% beta gap (-0.071 vs -0.064). Corrected to no year filter. Baseline spec: no controls, no weights. With controls+weights (Col 4): -0.075 (0.012). CS-DID ATT(simple)=-0.058 (gvar=207) vs TWFE=-0.064. 1716 rows with missing abortionRelated (132 per estado) dropped by att_gt. ESW weights at L_36 are ~0 (uninformative: single treated unit with 73 event-time dummies causes severe multicollinearity in weight decomposition).
